rulings.Case Title: JASON DEWITT VS. JACINTO JOSE VALDOVINO ET AL **Provide Access**
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, March 21, 2025, line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANT JACINTO VALDOVINO #AM3654 **PROVIDE ACCESS** Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint Off calendar. The moving party filed a notice of withdrawal of the motion on March 17, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN DE LEMOS VS. BISHOP FOX INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, March 21, 2025, line 3, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE On December 20, 2024, defendant's demurrer was on for hearing. The court continued the matter to January 17, 2025, for hearing and, in its minute order, ordered plaintiff to provide courtesy copies of its filing immediately with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Courtesy copies are required pursuant to Local Rule 2.7(B). On January 17, 2025, the matter was continued to February 6, 2025. On February 6, 2025, the court heard the demurrer, but, as noted in the court's minute order of that date, Plaintiff again failed to provide courtesy copies as required by Local Rule 2.7(B). The court issued an order to show cause which stated that "De Lemos has not provided the court with courtesy copies and the court therefore orders him to show cause why he should not be sanctioned in the amount of $250 for failure to comply with the court's December 20, 2024 order." The court set the OSC for hearing on March 21, 2025. Plaintiff has not shown any good cause or substantial justification for his failure to comply with the court's December 20, 2024 order. Accordingly, the court sanctions him in the amount of $250 pursuant to CCP 177.5. Sanctions shall be paid within 10 court days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL POWELL VS. YOUNG COMMUNITY DEVELOPERS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, March 21, 2025, line4, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT The City and County of San Francisco (City)'s unopposed demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The first amended complaint, filed on July 26, 2024, does not comply with CCP 425.10(a)(1), requiring a statement of the facts that would constitute a cause of action. Indeed, the complaint discloses no ultimate facts that would support relief on any theory. Plaintiff has not opposed the demurrer and has offered no facts he could plead to support relief. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL POWELL VS. ON WATCH GLOBAL LLC DBA ON WATCH LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, March 21, 2025, line5, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Defendant the City and County of San Francisco (City)'s unopposed demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The first amended complaint, filed on September 23, 2024, sues the City along with On Watch Global LLC and Walgreens. It purports to state a general negligence claim along with claims for gross negligence and intentional torts ("Stalking, Menacing, Assault" etc. (FAC at 3)). But the only fact it discloses is that "On 7/19/24, two Doe employees of defendant On Watch did willfully stalk, menace, assault, and attempt to assault with weapons, plaintiff Daniel Powell." (FAC at 3 para. 15 [the court has modified punctuation, spelling, and capitalizations to improve readability].) Nothing in this terse factual statement gives rise to any basis for holding the City liable. Plaintiff has filed no opposition, but the court reviewed his December 19, 2024 motion to strike the City's previous demurrer to discern whether Plaintiff is offering any explanation or facts he could assert on amendment. The court finds none. Accordingly the court denies leave to amend. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)
